description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 1.5 governance cluster: the end-to-end umbrella vs the standing permission matrix vs memory rules vs the compliance audit vs the Phase 1 stage skills.

cases.prompt: How should our humans and AI agents split the work of planning, implementing, reviewing, merging, and closing changes?
cases.reason: End-to-end lifecycle design is the umbrella; permissions and compliance are single columns inside it.

cases.prompt: Decide whether the agent is allowed to merge its own PRs to main once CI is green.
cases.reason: A standing authority decision for one action class is the matrix's cell, not a lifecycle redesign.

cases.prompt: Did last week's billing change follow our planning, approval, and validation process?
cases.reason: Judging one finished change against the process is the audit; the umbrella defines the process.

cases.prompt: Set the rules for what our agents may write into persistent project memory and when they must verify it.
cases.reason: Memory write/trust/hygiene rules are memory-governance's deliverable; the model only cites them in its learning loop.

cases.prompt: What validation does this RLS policy change need before it ships?
cases.reason: Routing one change to its validation floor is the classification gate working in-flight, not workflow design.
